channel.title: Artificial intelligence
channel.copyright: © Copyright The Financial Times Ltd 2025. "FT" and "Financial Times" are trademarks of the Financial Times. See http://www.ft.com/servicestools/help/terms#legal1 for the terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: ‘Do not trust your eyes’: AI generates surge in expense fraud
item.description: Business software groups warn that top AI models are increasingly being used to create ultra-realistic fake receipts

item.title: Nuclear treaties offer a blueprint for how to handle AI
item.description: The lack of co-ordinated efforts to address the existential risk of superintelligence is astonishing and must change

item.title: Submit your questions: Are markets in a bubble?
item.description: Take part in a live Ask an Expert Q&A with the FT’s markets columnist Katie Martin on Thursday October 30, at 1pm GMT

item.title: Will AI lengthen lifespans or shorten them?
item.description: New science advances may offer longer life to some, but the socio-economic effects may push others to die sooner

item.title: Criminals using AI are driving sharp rise in UK fraud cases
item.description: As part of a tech arms race, banks are also using artificial intelligence to stop scams with increasing effectiveness

item.title: Anthropic and Google Cloud strike blockbuster AI chips deal
item.description: Agreement between Claude chatbot maker and one of its largest investors will add to start-up’s computing firepower

item.title: OpenAI should make a phone
item.description: In order for artificial intelligence to live up to its promise, it must be part of the physical world around us

item.title: Crusoe raises $1.4bn as investors pile into AI data centres
item.description: Infrastructure provider to OpenAI triples valuation to $10bn in a year

item.title: How is AI reshaping the world of work? You asked, we answered
item.description: Chief data reporter John Burn-Murdoch and employment columnist Sarah O’Connor replied to readers

item.title: Where does Wikipedia go in the age of AI?
item.description: Crowdsourced, open edited and free, the site must seize the new technology as an opportunity

item.title: Where are all the job losses?
item.description: A macroeconomic change isn’t clear yet but payroll data shows some types of work are already being displaced

item.title: AI tutors show their potential in interactive workplace learning
item.description: As bots transform routine training and democratise corporate coaching, humans remain the experts in sensitive areas

item.title: AI’s rapid evolution demands more flexible training
item.description: Businesses risk losing competitiveness unless they frequently renew learning programmes

item.title: Jobseekers of the future: approach AI with scepticism and dexterity
item.description: A key skill for the future will be understanding what AI can — and cannot — do

item.title: Reddit sues AI search engine Perplexity for scraping its data
item.description: Social media group claims the start-up harvested user conversations to train its artificial intelligence models

item.title: OpenAI prioritised user engagement over suicide prevention, lawsuit claims
item.description: Family of teen who took his own life after ChatGPT use alleges chatbot maker intentionally weakened protections

item.title: How to revive your team — and your tech rollout
item.description: Look to sales operations staff for the skills and mindset to boost the rest of the business

item.title: Short sellers blame retail investors for worst returns since 2020
item.description: Rally in heavily shorted stocks comes as AI hype and hopes of lower rates push S&P 500 to record highs

item.title: How employers can prevent AI ‘work slop’
item.description: Poorly generated content underlines need for clear policies and increased monitoring of technology

item.title: In the age of AI, what should your kids study at university?
item.description: Artificial intelligence is changing the calculus when it comes to career paybacks

item.title: Why has US jobs growth been so anaemic?
item.description: No, it's not AI

item.title: Successful AI adoption needs workers in the loop
item.description: Companies must rethink what AI will do to their relevance

item.title: Bannon and Markle among 800 public figures calling for AI ‘superintelligence’ ban
item.description: Politicians, corporate bosses, tech experts, celebrities and religious leaders call for ‘prohibition’ on advanced AI systems

item.title: The fallout from the AI-fuelled dash for gas
item.description: A supply crunch in giant turbines for gas-powered plants threatens environmental and geopolitical consequences

item.title: OpenAI launches Atlas web browser
item.description: ChatGPT-maker’s tool will integrate popular chatbot in challenge to Google and Microsoft
